channel.title: Burp-Suite on Robert Akhmerov | v31dt
channel.description: Recent content in Burp-Suite on Robert Akhmerov | v31dt

item.title: KBC Offensive Security Internship
item.description:
Context

From February 2026 to May 2026, I completed an offensive security internship at KBC. The work focused on mobile penetration testing, Android banking malware research, and automation for security testing environments.

Technologies

  • Burp Suite
  • Frida
  • OWASP MASVS
  • Android security testing
  • Malware analysis tooling
  • Automation scripting

My Contribution

I developed automation for mobile penetration testing environments, integrated Burp Suite with Frida for login flow automation, contributed to a KBC mobile security assessment based on OWASP MASVS, and researched Android banking malware.

item.title: AInalyzer
item.description:
Context

AInalyzer is an open-source AI-powered Burp Suite extension. It integrates an AI assistant into HTTP endpoint security investigations while keeping a human in the loop.

Project repository: github.com/v31dt/ainalyzer

It supports OpenAI-compatible chat completion APIs, local OpenAI-compatible servers such as Ollama or LM Studio, OpenAI directly, and coding agents through AgentAPI. The AgentAPI mode can connect the extension to tools such as Codex, Claude Code, Gemini, Aider, or Goose.

item.title: ELK + Burp Suite Monitoring System
item.description:
Context

This project built a Burp Suite and ELK Stack setup for centralized storage, analysis, and anomaly detection of HTTP requests.

Project report: Anomaly Detection and Historical Analysis of Burp Suite Requests

Technologies

  • Burp Suite
  • Elasticsearch
  • Logstash / Beats
  • Kibana
  • Filebeat
  • HTTP request analysis

Result

The setup supports security research and vulnerability tracking by collecting and analyzing HTTP traffic in a centralized monitoring stack.

Reflection

This project connected penetration testing workflows with monitoring and analysis. It pushed me to think beyond individual findings and look at how request data can be stored, searched, and used during longer security investigations.
